messages.content:
Discuss how file descriptor exhaustion relates to this vulnerability scenario.

Go before 1.16.12 and 1.17.x before 1.17.5 on UNIX allows write operations to an unintended file or unintended network connection as a consequence of erroneous closing of file descriptor 0 after file-descriptor exhaustion.

File descriptor exhaustion occurs when the system runs out of available file descriptors, causing processes to malfunction. In this scenario, it results in file descriptor 0 being closed improperly, allowing unintentional write operations to occur.
